objects.name: Sol
objects.description: The central star of the Solar System, providing light and heat to all orbiting worlds.

objects.name: Mercury
objects.description: The smallest planet, closest to Sol, experiencing extreme temperature variations.

objects.name: Venus
objects.description: A terrestrial planet enveloped in a dense, carbon dioxide-rich atmosphere with runaway greenhouse temperatures.

objects.name: Earth
objects.description: Our home planet, a vibrant blue world rich in water, oxygen, and diverse life.

objects.name: Luna
objects.description: Earth's single natural satellite, responsible for tidal locks and ocean movements.

objects.name: Mars
objects.description: The Red Planet, characterized by iron oxide soil, polar ice caps, and historical water channels.

objects.name: Phobos
objects.description: The larger and inner moon of Mars, orbiting extremely close to its parent body.

objects.name: Deimos
objects.description: The smaller and outer moon of Mars, taking a longer orbit around the red planet.

objects.name: Jupiter
objects.description: The largest planet in the system, a gas giant with massive storms and a powerful magnetic field.
